test_family: Granger causality, signal layers (L1/L2/L3) for 서울특별시

composite.note: Composite is a single national-aggregate test, not part of the multi-test family.

data_gap.issue: 17-region per-region Granger p-values are not available in canonical artifacts.
data_gap.consequence: We cannot run a 17-region multiple-testing correction without re-running analysis/lead_time_2025w48.py against all 17 regions. The V11 backtest metrics (recall/F1/FAR/MCC) are per-region, but Granger causality was only computed for the Seoul case study.
data_gap.next_step: If reviewers require per-region Granger correction, extend analysis/lead_time_2025w48.py to iterate over all 17 regions and re-run; rerun this script against the new p-vector.

interpretation: Across the 3 signal layers (L1 OTC / L2 wastewater / L3 search) for 서울특별시, 1/3 are significant at raw α=0.05, 1/3 survive Bonferroni, and 1/3 survive BH-FDR. The composite (national-aggregate) p=0.0209 is significant on its own and is reported outside the multi-test family because it is a single derived test, not one of many. L2 (wastewater) fails all corrections — consistent with the small sample (12 weeks) and the ongoing data-coverage limitation flagged in the V11 metric notes.
